/.vercel/README.txt:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1 | > Why do I have a folder named ".vercel" in my project?
  2 | The ".vercel" folder is created when you link a directory to a Vercel project.
  3 | 
  4 | > What does the "project.json" file contain?
  5 | The "project.json" file contains:
  6 | - The ID of the Vercel project that you linked ("projectId")
  7 | - The ID of the user or team your Vercel project is owned by ("orgId")
  8 | 
  9 | > Should I commit the ".vercel" folder?
 10 | No, you should not share the ".vercel" folder with anyone.
 11 | Upon creation, it will be automatically added to your ".gitignore" file.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

/.vercel/cache/b_send_slack/.github/workflows/ping.yml:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1 | name: Ping API every hour
  2 | 
  3 | on:
  4 |   schedule:
  5 |     - cron: "0 * * * *"  # 毎時0分
  6 | 
  7 | jobs:
  8 |   ping:
  9 |     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
 10 |     steps:
 11 |       - name: Call your endpoint
 12 |         run: |
 13 |           curl -X GET "https://makers-release.vercel.app/b_send_slack"
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

 49 | Werkzeug
 50 | ========
 51 | 
 52 | *werkzeug* German noun: "tool". Etymology: *werk* ("work"), *zeug* ("stuff")
 53 | 
 54 | Werkzeug is a comprehensive `WSGI`_ web application library. It began as
 55 | a simple collection of various utilities for WSGI applications and has
 56 | become one of the most advanced WSGI utility libraries.
 57 | 
 58 | It includes:
 59 | 
 60 | -   An interactive debugger that allows inspecting stack traces and
 61 |     source code in the browser with an interactive interpreter for any
 62 |     frame in the stack.
 63 | -   A full-featured request object with objects to interact with
 64 |     headers, query args, form data, files, and cookies.
 65 | -   A response object that can wrap other WSGI applications and handle
 66 |     streaming data.
 67 | -   A routing system for matching URLs to endpoints and generating URLs
 68 |     for endpoints, with an extensible system for capturing variables
 69 |     from URLs.
 70 | -   HTTP utilities to handle entity tags, cache control, dates, user
 71 |     agents, cookies, files, and more.
 72 | -   A threaded WSGI server for use while developing applications
 73 |     locally.
 74 | -   A test client for simulating HTTP requests during testing without
 75 |     requiring running a server.
 76 | 
 77 | Werkzeug is Unicode aware and doesn't enforce any dependencies. It is up
 78 | to the developer to choose a template engine, database adapter, and even
 79 | how to handle requests. It can be used to build all sorts of end user
 80 | applications such as blogs, wikis, or bulletin boards.
 81 | 
 82 | `Flask`_ wraps Werkzeug, using it to handle the details of WSGI while
 83 | providing more structure and patterns for defining powerful
 84 | applications.
 85 | 
 86 | 
 87 | Installing
 88 | ----------
 89 | 
 90 | Install and update using `pip`_:
 91 | 
 92 | .. code-block:: text
 93 | 
 94 |     pip install -U Werkzeug
 95 | 
 96 | 
 97 | A Simple Example
 98 | ----------------
 99 | 
100 | .. code-block:: python
101 | 
102 |     from werkzeug.wrappers import Request, Response
103 | 
104 |     @Request.application
105 |     def application(request):
106 |         return Response('Hello, World!')
107 | 
108 |     if __name__ == '__main__':
109 |         from werkzeug.serving import run_simple
110 |         run_simple('localhost', 4000, application)
111 | 
112 |

117 | -   Documentation: https://werkzeug.palletsprojects.com/
118 | -   Releases: https://pypi.org/project/Werkzeug/
119 | -   Code: https://github.com/pallets/werkzeug
120 | -   Issue tracker: https://github.com/pallets/werkzeug/issues

/.vercel/cache/b_send_slack/api/a1_check_releace:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1 | import json
  2 | import re
  3 | import sys
  4 | import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
  5 | from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
  6 | from pathlib import Path
  7 | from typing import Any, Dict, List
  8 | 
  9 | import requests
 10 | 
 11 | BASE_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
 12 | if str(BASE_DIR) not in sys.path:
 13 |     sys.path.append(str(BASE_DIR))
 14 | 
 15 | from spreadsheet2json import load_spreadsheet_data
 16 | 
 17 | SITEMAP_URL = "https://prtimes.jp/sitemap-news.xml"
 18 | OUTPUT_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "output"
 19 | OUTPUT_PATH = OUTPUT_DIR / "a1_check_releace.json"
 20 | 
 21 | 
 22 | def load_prtimes_ids() -> List[str]:
 23 |     """Load company_id list from spreadsheet2json output."""
 24 |     # 本番でも毎回スプレッドシートを読み直す
 25 |     data = load_spreadsheet_data(force_refresh=True, persist=False)
 26 |     raw_ids = data.get("prtimes_id", []) if isinstance(data, dict) else []
 27 |     ids: List[str] = []
 28 |     for cid in raw_ids:
 29 |         cid_str = str(cid).strip()
 30 |         if not cid_str:
 31 |             continue
 32 |         ids.append(cid_str.lstrip("0") or "0")
 33 |     return ids
 34 | 
 35 | 
 36 | def fetch_sitemap() -> bytes:
 37 |     """Fetch the news sitemap (contains latest press releases across PR TIMES)."""
 38 |     resp = requests.get(SITEMAP_URL, timeout=15)
 39 |     resp.raise_for_status()
 40 |     return resp.content
 41 | 
 42 | 
 43 | def parse_sitemap(xml_bytes: bytes) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
 44 |     """Parse sitemap XML and return list of releases with company_id."""
 45 |     ns = {
 46 |         "sm": "http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9",
 47 |         "news": "http://www.google.com/schemas/sitemap-news/0.9",
 48 |     }
 49 |     root = ET.fromstring(xml_bytes)
 50 |     releases: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
 51 |     pattern = re.compile(r"/p/(\d+)\.(\d+)\.html")
 52 | 
 53 |     for url_node in root.findall("sm:url", ns):
 54 |         loc_el = url_node.find("sm:loc", ns)
 55 |         news_el = url_node.find("news:news", ns)
 56 |         if loc_el is None or news_el is None:
 57 |             continue
 58 | 
 59 |         loc = (loc_el.text or "").strip()
 60 |         m = pattern.search(loc)
 61 |         if not m:
 62 |             continue
 63 | 
 64 |         company_id = m.group(2).lstrip("0") or "0"
 65 |         pub_date_el = news_el.find("news:publication_date", ns)
 66 |         title_el = news_el.find("news:title", ns)
 67 |         if pub_date_el is None:
 68 |             continue
 69 | 
 70 |         pub_raw = (pub_date_el.text or "").strip()
 71 |         try:
 72 |             published_at = datetime.fromisoformat(pub_raw)
 73 |         except ValueError:
 74 |             continue
 75 | 
 76 |         releases.append(
 77 |             {
 78 |                 "company_id": company_id,
 79 |                 "url": loc,
 80 |                 "title": (title_el.text or "").strip() if title_el is not None else "",
 81 |                 "published_at": published_at,
 82 |             }
 83 |         )
 84 |     return releases
 85 | 
 86 | 
 87 | def filter_recent_releases(
 88 |     releases: List[Dict[str, Any]],
 89 |     target_ids: List[str],
 90 |     window_hours: int = 1,
 91 | ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
 92 |     """Filter releases to target company IDs within the last window_hours."""
 93 |     target_set = set(target_ids)
 94 |     now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
 95 |     window_start = now - timedelta(hours=window_hours)
 96 |     filtered: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
 97 | 
 98 |     for r in releases:
 99 |         cid = r["company_id"]
100 |         pub_dt: datetime = r["published_at"]
101 |         pub_dt_utc = pub_dt.astimezone(timezone.utc)
102 | 
103 |         if cid not in target_set:
104 |             continue
105 |         if pub_dt_utc < window_start:
106 |             continue
107 | 
108 |         filtered.append(
109 |             {
110 |                 "company_id": cid,
111 |                 "url": r["url"],
112 |                 "title": r["title"],
113 |                 "published_at": pub_dt_utc.isoformat(),
114 |             }
115 |         )
116 | 
117 |     filtered.sort(key=lambda x: x["published_at"], reverse=True)
118 |     return filtered
119 | 
120 | 
121 | def check_releases(window_hours: int = 1) -> Dict[str, Any]:
122 |     """High-level entry: fetch sitemap, filter, and return structured data."""
123 |     ids = load_prtimes_ids()
124 |     xml_bytes = fetch_sitemap()
125 |     releases = parse_sitemap(xml_bytes)
126 |     recent = filter_recent_releases(releases, ids, window_hours=window_hours)
127 | 
128 |     now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
129 |     payload: Dict[str, Any] = {
130 |         "checked_at": now.isoformat(),
131 |         "window_hours": window_hours,
132 |         "target_ids": ids,
133 |         "count": len(recent),
134 |         "releases": recent,
135 |     }
136 |     return payload
137 | 
138 | 
139 | def write_output(data: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
140 |     try:
141 |         OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
142 |         OUTPUT_PATH.write_text(json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
143 |     except OSError:
144 |         # Skip persist on read-only filesystems (e.g., serverless)
145 |         pass
146 | 
147 | 
148 | def main():
149 |     data = check_releases(window_hours=1)
150 |     write_output(data)
151 |     print(f"Saved {data['count']} releases to {OUTPUT_PATH}")
152 | 
153 | 
154 | if __name__ == "__main__":
155 |     main()
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

/.vercel/cache/b_send_slack/api/a1_check_releace.py:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1 | import json
  2 | import re
  3 | import sys
  4 | import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
  5 | from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
  6 | from pathlib import Path
  7 | from typing import Any, Dict, List
  8 | 
  9 | import requests
 10 | 
 11 | BASE_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
 12 | if str(BASE_DIR) not in sys.path:
 13 |     sys.path.append(str(BASE_DIR))
 14 | 
 15 | from spreadsheet2json import load_spreadsheet_data
 16 | 
 17 | SITEMAP_URL = "https://prtimes.jp/sitemap-news.xml"
 18 | OUTPUT_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "output"
 19 | OUTPUT_PATH = OUTPUT_DIR / "a1_check_releace.json"
 20 | 
 21 | 
 22 | def load_prtimes_ids() -> List[str]:
 23 |     """Load company_id list from spreadsheet2json output."""
 24 |     # 本番でも毎回スプレッドシートを読み直す
 25 |     data = load_spreadsheet_data(force_refresh=True, persist=False)
 26 |     raw_ids = data.get("prtimes_id", []) if isinstance(data, dict) else []
 27 |     ids: List[str] = []
 28 |     for cid in raw_ids:
 29 |         cid_str = str(cid).strip()
 30 |         if not cid_str:
 31 |             continue
 32 |         ids.append(cid_str.lstrip("0") or "0")
 33 |     return ids
 34 | 
 35 | 
 36 | def fetch_sitemap() -> bytes:
 37 |     """Fetch the news sitemap (contains latest press releases across PR TIMES)."""
 38 |     resp = requests.get(SITEMAP_URL, timeout=15)
 39 |     resp.raise_for_status()
 40 |     return resp.content
 41 | 
 42 | 
 43 | def parse_sitemap(xml_bytes: bytes) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
 44 |     """Parse sitemap XML and return list of releases with company_id."""
 45 |     ns = {
 46 |         "sm": "http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9",
 47 |         "news": "http://www.google.com/schemas/sitemap-news/0.9",
 48 |     }
 49 |     root = ET.fromstring(xml_bytes)
 50 |     releases: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
 51 |     pattern = re.compile(r"/p/(\d+)\.(\d+)\.html")
 52 | 
 53 |     for url_node in root.findall("sm:url", ns):
 54 |         loc_el = url_node.find("sm:loc", ns)
 55 |         news_el = url_node.find("news:news", ns)
 56 |         if loc_el is None or news_el is None:
 57 |             continue
 58 | 
 59 |         loc = (loc_el.text or "").strip()
 60 |         m = pattern.search(loc)
 61 |         if not m:
 62 |             continue
 63 | 
 64 |         company_id = m.group(2).lstrip("0") or "0"
 65 |         pub_date_el = news_el.find("news:publication_date", ns)
 66 |         title_el = news_el.find("news:title", ns)
 67 |         if pub_date_el is None:
 68 |             continue
 69 | 
 70 |         pub_raw = (pub_date_el.text or "").strip()
 71 |         try:
 72 |             published_at = datetime.fromisoformat(pub_raw)
 73 |         except ValueError:
 74 |             continue
 75 | 
 76 |         releases.append(
 77 |             {
 78 |                 "company_id": company_id,
 79 |                 "url": loc,
 80 |                 "title": (title_el.text or "").strip() if title_el is not None else "",
 81 |                 "published_at": published_at,
 82 |             }
 83 |         )
 84 |     return releases
 85 | 
 86 | 
 87 | def filter_recent_releases(
 88 |     releases: List[Dict[str, Any]],
 89 |     target_ids: List[str],
 90 |     window_hours: int = 1,
 91 | ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
 92 |     """Filter releases to target company IDs within the last window_hours."""
 93 |     target_set = set(target_ids)
 94 |     now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
 95 |     window_start = now - timedelta(hours=window_hours)
 96 |     filtered: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
 97 | 
 98 |     for r in releases:
 99 |         cid = r["company_id"]
100 |         pub_dt: datetime = r["published_at"]
101 |         pub_dt_utc = pub_dt.astimezone(timezone.utc)
102 | 
103 |         if cid not in target_set:
104 |             continue
105 |         if pub_dt_utc < window_start:
106 |             continue
107 | 
108 |         filtered.append(
109 |             {
110 |                 "company_id": cid,
111 |                 "url": r["url"],
112 |                 "title": r["title"],
113 |                 "published_at": pub_dt_utc.isoformat(),
114 |             }
115 |         )
116 | 
117 |     filtered.sort(key=lambda x: x["published_at"], reverse=True)
118 |     return filtered
119 | 
120 | 
121 | def check_releases(window_hours: int = 1) -> Dict[str, Any]:
122 |     """High-level entry: fetch sitemap, filter, and return structured data."""
123 |     ids = load_prtimes_ids()
124 |     xml_bytes = fetch_sitemap()
125 |     releases = parse_sitemap(xml_bytes)
126 |     recent = filter_recent_releases(releases, ids, window_hours=window_hours)
127 | 
128 |     now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
129 |     payload: Dict[str, Any] = {
130 |         "checked_at": now.isoformat(),
131 |         "window_hours": window_hours,
132 |         "target_ids": ids,
133 |         "count": len(recent),
134 |         "releases": recent,
135 |     }
136 |     return payload
137 | 
138 | 
139 | def write_output(data: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
140 |     try:
141 |         OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
142 |         OUTPUT_PATH.write_text(json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
143 |     except OSError:
144 |         # Skip persist on read-only filesystems (e.g., serverless)
145 |         pass
146 | 
147 | 
148 | def main():
149 |     data = check_releases(window_hours=1)
150 |     write_output(data)
151 |     print(f"Saved {data['count']} releases to {OUTPUT_PATH}")
152 | 
153 | 
154 | if __name__ == "__main__":
155 |     main()
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

/.vercel/cache/b_send_slack/api/a2_check_note:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1 | import json
  2 | import sys
  3 | import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
  4 | from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
  5 | from email.utils import parsedate_to_datetime
  6 | from pathlib import Path
  7 | from typing import Any, Dict, List
  8 | from urllib.parse import urlparse
  9 | 
 10 | import requests
 11 | 
 12 | BASE_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
 13 | if str(BASE_DIR) not in sys.path:
 14 |     sys.path.append(str(BASE_DIR))
 15 | 
 16 | from spreadsheet2json import load_spreadsheet_data
 17 | 
 18 | OUTPUT_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "output"
 19 | OUTPUT_PATH = OUTPUT_DIR / "a2_check_note.py.json"
 20 | FEED_URL_TEMPLATE = "https://note.com/{note_id}/rss"
 21 | HTTP_HEADERS = {
 22 |     "User-Agent": (
 23 |         "Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_15_7) "
 24 |         "AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) "
 25 |         "Chrome/124.0.0.0 Safari/537.36"
 26 |     )
 27 | }
 28 | 
 29 | 
 30 | def _normalize_note_id(value: str) -> str:
 31 |     """Extract note username/slug from URL or raw value."""
 32 |     raw = value.strip()
 33 |     if not raw:
 34 |         return ""
 35 | 
 36 |     if "note.com" in raw or "://" in raw:
 37 |         parsed = urlparse(raw if "://" in raw else f"https://{raw}")
 38 |         path = parsed.path.strip("/")
 39 |         if not path:
 40 |             return ""
 41 |         raw = path.split("/")[0]
 42 | 
 43 |     return raw.lstrip("@")
 44 | 
 45 | 
 46 | def load_note_ids() -> List[str]:
 47 |     """Load note user IDs from spreadsheet2json output."""
 48 |     # 本番でも毎回スプレッドシートを読み直す
 49 |     data = load_spreadsheet_data(force_refresh=True, persist=False)
 50 |     raw_ids = data.get("note_id", []) if isinstance(data, dict) else []
 51 |     ids: List[str] = []
 52 |     for note_id in raw_ids:
 53 |         normalized = _normalize_note_id(str(note_id))
 54 |         if normalized:
 55 |             ids.append(normalized)
 56 |     return ids
 57 | 
 58 | 
 59 | def fetch_rss(note_id: str) -> bytes:
 60 |     """Fetch RSS feed bytes for a given note user."""
 61 |     url = FEED_URL_TEMPLATE.format(note_id=note_id)
 62 |     resp = requests.get(url, headers=HTTP_HEADERS, timeout=15)
 63 |     resp.raise_for_status()
 64 |     return resp.content
 65 | 
 66 | 
 67 | def parse_rss(xml_bytes: bytes, note_id: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
 68 |     """Parse RSS XML and return articles for the specified note_id."""
 69 |     try:
 70 |         root = ET.fromstring(xml_bytes)
 71 |     except ET.ParseError:
 72 |         return []
 73 | 
 74 |     channel = root.find("channel")
 75 |     if channel is None:
 76 |         return []
 77 | 
 78 |     items: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
 79 |     for item in channel.findall("item"):
 80 |         title = (item.findtext("title") or "").strip()
 81 |         link = (item.findtext("link") or "").strip()
 82 |         pub_raw = (item.findtext("pubDate") or "").strip()
 83 |         if not link or not pub_raw:
 84 |             continue
 85 | 
 86 |         try:
 87 |             pub_dt = parsedate_to_datetime(pub_raw)
 88 |         except (TypeError, ValueError):
 89 |             continue
 90 | 
 91 |         if pub_dt.tzinfo is None:
 92 |             pub_dt = pub_dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
 93 | 
 94 |         items.append(
 95 |             {
 96 |                 "note_id": note_id,
 97 |                 "url": link,
 98 |                 "title": title,
 99 |                 "published_at": pub_dt,
100 |             }
101 |         )
102 |     return items
103 | 
104 | 
105 | def filter_recent(articles: List[Dict[str, Any]], window_hours: int = 1) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
106 |     """Return articles published within the last window_hours."""
107 |     now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
108 |     window_start = now - timedelta(hours=window_hours)
109 | 
110 |     filtered: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
111 |     for article in articles:
112 |         pub_dt: datetime = article["published_at"]
113 |         pub_dt_utc = pub_dt.astimezone(timezone.utc)
114 |         if pub_dt_utc < window_start:
115 |             continue
116 | 
117 |         filtered.append(
118 |             {
119 |                 "note_id": article["note_id"],
120 |                 "url": article["url"],
121 |                 "title": article["title"],
122 |                 "published_at": pub_dt_utc.isoformat(),
123 |             }
124 |         )
125 | 
126 |     filtered.sort(key=lambda x: x["published_at"], reverse=True)
127 |     return filtered
128 | 
129 | 
130 | def check_notes(window_hours: int = 1) -> Dict[str, Any]:
131 |     """High-level entry: fetch RSS feeds, filter recent, and return structured data."""
132 |     note_ids = load_note_ids()
133 |     all_articles: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
134 | 
135 |     for note_id in note_ids:
136 |         try:
137 |             xml_bytes = fetch_rss(note_id)
138 |             all_articles.extend(parse_rss(xml_bytes, note_id))
139 |         except Exception:
140 |             continue
141 | 
142 |     recent = filter_recent(all_articles, window_hours=window_hours)
143 |     now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
144 |     payload: Dict[str, Any] = {
145 |         "checked_at": now.isoformat(),
146 |         "window_hours": window_hours,
147 |         "target_ids": note_ids,
148 |         "count": len(recent),
149 |         "articles": recent,
150 |     }
151 |     return payload
152 | 
153 | 
154 | def write_output(data: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
155 |     try:
156 |         OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
157 |         OUTPUT_PATH.write_text(json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
158 |     except OSError:
159 |         # Skip persist on read-only filesystems (e.g., serverless)
160 |         pass
161 | 
162 | 
163 | def main():
164 |     data = check_notes(window_hours=1)
165 |     write_output(data)
166 |     print(f"Saved {data['count']} articles to {OUTPUT_PATH}")
167 | 
168 | 
169 | if __name__ == "__main__":
170 |     main()
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  5 | from email.utils import parsedate_to_datetime
  6 | from pathlib import Path

 16 | 
 17 | OUTPUT_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "output"
 18 | OUTPUT_PATH = OUTPUT_DIR / "a3_check_x.json"
 19 | FEED_URL_TEMPLATE = "https://nitter.net/{x_id}/rss"

 28 | 
 29 | def load_x_ids() -> List[str]:
 30 |     """Load X(Twitter) user IDs from spreadsheet2json output."""
 31 |     # 本番でも毎回スプレッドシートを読み直す
 32 |     data = load_spreadsheet_data(force_refresh=True, persist=False)
 33 |     raw_ids = data.get("x_id", []) if isinstance(data, dict) else []
 34 |     ids: List[str] = []
 35 |     for x_id in raw_ids:
 36 |         x_str = str(x_id).strip()
 37 |         if x_str:
 38 |             ids.append(x_str)
 39 |     return ids
 40 | 
 41 | 
 42 | def fetch_rss(x_id: str) -> bytes:
 43 |     """Fetch RSS feed bytes for a given X user via Nitter."""
 44 |     url = FEED_URL_TEMPLATE.format(x_id=x_id)
 45 |     resp = requests.get(url, headers=HTTP_HEADERS, timeout=15)
 46 |     resp.raise_for_status()
 47 |     return resp.content
 48 | 
 49 | 
 50 | def parse_rss(xml_bytes: bytes, x_id: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
 51 |     """Parse RSS XML and return tweets for the specified x_id."""
 52 |     try:
 53 |         root = ET.fromstring(xml_bytes)
 54 |     except ET.ParseError:
 55 |         return []
 56 | 
 57 |     channel = root.find("channel")
 58 |     if channel is None:
 59 |         return []
 60 | 
 61 |     tweets: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
 62 |     for item in channel.findall("item"):
 63 |         title = (item.findtext("title") or "").strip()
 64 |         link = (item.findtext("link") or "").strip()
 65 |         pub_raw = (item.findtext("pubDate") or "").strip()
 66 |         if not link or not pub_raw:
 67 |             continue
 68 | 
 69 |         try:
 70 |             pub_dt = parsedate_to_datetime(pub_raw)
 71 |         except (TypeError, ValueError):
 72 |             continue
 73 | 
 74 |         if pub_dt.tzinfo is None:
 75 |             pub_dt = pub_dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
 76 | 
 77 |         tweets.append(
 78 |             {
 79 |                 "x_id": x_id,
 80 |                 "url": link,
 81 |                 "title": title,
 82 |                 "published_at": pub_dt,
 83 |             }
 84 |         )
 85 |     return tweets
 86 | 
 87 | 
 88 | def filter_recent(tweets: List[Dict[str, Any]], window_hours: int = 1) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
 89 |     """Return tweets published within the last window_hours."""
 90 |     now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
 91 |     window_start = now - timedelta(hours=window_hours)
 92 | 
 93 |     filtered: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
 94 |     for tweet in tweets:
 95 |         pub_dt: datetime = tweet["published_at"]
 96 |         pub_dt_utc = pub_dt.astimezone(timezone.utc)
 97 |         if pub_dt_utc < window_start:
 98 |             continue
 99 | 
100 |         filtered.append(
101 |             {
102 |                 "x_id": tweet["x_id"],
103 |                 "url": tweet["url"],
104 |                 "title": tweet["title"],
105 |                 "published_at": pub_dt_utc.isoformat(),
106 |             }
107 |         )
108 | 
109 |     filtered.sort(key=lambda x: x["published_at"], reverse=True)
110 |     return filtered
111 | 
112 | 
113 | def check_x(window_hours: int = 1) -> Dict[str, Any]:
114 |     """High-level entry: fetch Nitter feeds, filter recent, and return structured data."""
115 |     x_ids = load_x_ids()
116 |     all_tweets: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
117 | 
118 |     for x_id in x_ids:
119 |         try:
120 |             xml_bytes = fetch_rss(x_id)
121 |             all_tweets.extend(parse_rss(xml_bytes, x_id))
122 |         except Exception:
123 |             continue
124 | 
125 |     recent = filter_recent(all_tweets, window_hours=window_hours)
126 |     now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
127 |     payload: Dict[str, Any] = {
128 |         "checked_at": now.isoformat(),
129 |         "window_hours": window_hours,
130 |         "target_ids": x_ids,
131 |         "count": len(recent),
132 |         "tweets": recent,
133 |     }
134 |     return payload
135 | 
136 | 
137 | def write_output(data: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
138 |     try:
139 |         OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
140 |         OUTPUT_PATH.write_text(json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
141 |     except OSError:
142 |         # Skip persist on read-only filesystems (e.g., serverless)
143 |         pass
144 | 
145 | 
146 | def main():
147 |     data = check_x(window_hours=1)
148 |     write_output(data)
149 |     print(f"Saved {data['count']} tweets to {OUTPUT_PATH}")
150 | 
151 | 
152 | if __name__ == "__main__":
153 |     main()
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

 21 |     "User-Agent": (
 22 |         "Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_15_7) "

 29 | def load_x_ids() -> List[str]:
 30 |     """Load X(Twitter) user IDs from spreadsheet2json output."""
 31 |     # 本番でも毎回スプレッドシートを読み直す
 32 |     data = load_spreadsheet_data(force_refresh=True, persist=False)
 33 |     raw_ids = data.get("x_id", []) if isinstance(data, dict) else []
 34 |     ids: List[str] = []
 35 |     for x_id in raw_ids:
 36 |         x_str = str(x_id).strip()
 37 |         if x_str:
 38 |             ids.append(x_str)
 39 |     return ids

/.vercel/cache/b_send_slack/api/b_send_slack:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1 | import os
  2 | import json
  3 | import sys
  4 | from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
  5 | from pathlib import Path
  6 | from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
  7 | import re
  8 | from http.server import BaseHTTPRequestHandler
  9 | 
 10 | import requests
 11 | 
 12 | BASE_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
 13 | if str(BASE_DIR) not in sys.path:
 14 |     sys.path.append(str(BASE_DIR))
 15 | 
 16 | import api.a2_check_note as note_checker
 17 | import api.a1_check_releace as pr_checker
 18 | from spreadsheet2json import load_spreadsheet_data
 19 | 
 20 | 
 21 | def _build_name_index(sheet: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, str]]:
 22 |     """Create lookup tables to resolve names by prtimes_id / note_id / x_id."""
 23 |     by_name = sheet.get("by_name") if isinstance(sheet, dict) else {}
 24 |     pr_by_id: Dict[str, str] = {}
 25 |     note_by_id: Dict[str, str] = {}
 26 |     x_by_id: Dict[str, str] = {}
 27 |     if not isinstance(by_name, dict):
 28 |         return {"pr": pr_by_id, "note": note_by_id, "x": x_by_id}
 29 | 
 30 |     for name, ids in by_name.items():
 31 |         if not isinstance(ids, dict):
 32 |             continue
 33 |         pr_id = str(ids.get("prtimes_id", "")).strip()
 34 |         if pr_id:
 35 |             pr_by_id[pr_id.lstrip("0") or "0"] = name
 36 | 
 37 |         note_id = str(ids.get("note_id", "")).strip()
 38 |         if note_id:
 39 |             note_by_id[note_id] = name
 40 | 
 41 |         x_id = str(ids.get("x_id", "")).strip()
 42 |         if x_id:
 43 |             x_by_id[x_id] = name
 44 | 
 45 |     return {"pr": pr_by_id, "note": note_by_id, "x": x_by_id}
 46 | 
 47 | 
 48 | def _load_env_file():
 49 |     """
 50 |     Minimal .env loader for local runs (KEY="value" lines only).
 51 |     Safe to call in any environment.
 52 |     """
 53 |     env_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / ".env"
 54 |     if not env_path.exists():
 55 |         return
 56 | 
 57 |     with env_path.open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
 58 |         for line in f:
 59 |             line = line.strip()
 60 |             if not line or line.startswith("#") or "=" not in line:
 61 |                 continue
 62 |             key, val = line.split("=", 1)
 63 |             val = val.strip().strip('"').strip("'")
 64 |             os.environ.setdefault(key, val)
 65 | 
 66 | 
 67 | _load_env_file()
 68 | 
 69 | SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL = os.environ.get("SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL")
 70 | HTTP_HEADERS = {
 71 |     "User-Agent": (
 72 |         "Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_15_7) "
 73 |         "AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) "
 74 |         "Chrome/124.0.0.0 Safari/537.36"
 75 |     )
 76 | }
 77 | 
 78 | 
 79 | def send_to_slack(
 80 |     text: str,
 81 |     *,
 82 |     enable_unfurl: bool = True,
 83 |     blocks: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None,
 84 | ) -> requests.Response:
 85 |     if not SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL:
 86 |         raise RuntimeError("SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL is not set")
 87 | 
 88 |     payload = {
 89 |         "text": text,
 90 |         "unfurl_links": enable_unfurl,
 91 |         "unfurl_media": enable_unfurl,
 92 |     }
 93 |     if blocks:
 94 |         payload["blocks"] = blocks
 95 | 
 96 |     resp = requests.post(SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL, json=payload, timeout=10)
 97 |     resp.raise_for_status()
 98 |     return resp
 99 | 
100 | 
101 | def _extract_meta(content: str, pattern: str) -> str:
102 |     """Return first capture from regex pattern (dotall)."""
103 |     m = re.search(pattern, content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
104 |     return (m.group(1).strip() if m else "").replace("\n", " ").strip()
105 | 
106 | 
107 | def fetch_preview(url: str) -> Dict[str, str]:
108 |     """
109 |     Fetch page and extract og:title / og:description / title.
110 |     Best-effort; returns empty strings on failure.
111 |     """
112 |     try:
113 |         resp = requests.get(url, headers=HTTP_HEADERS, timeout=10)
114 |         resp.raise_for_status()
115 |         resp.encoding = resp.apparent_encoding or resp.encoding or "utf-8"
116 |         html = resp.text
117 |     except Exception:
118 |         return {"title": "", "description": ""}
119 | 
120 |     og_title = _extract_meta(
121 |         html, r'<meta[^>]+property=["\']og:title["\'][^>]+content=["\'](.*?)["\']'
122 |     )
123 |     og_desc = _extract_meta(
124 |         html, r'<meta[^>]+property=["\']og:description["\'][^>]+content=["\'](.*?)["\']'
125 |     )
126 |     og_image = _extract_meta(
127 |         html, r'<meta[^>]+property=["\']og:image["\'][^>]+content=["\'](.*?)["\']'
128 |     )
129 |     title_tag = _extract_meta(html, r"<title[^>]*>(.*?)</title>")
130 | 
131 |     title = og_title or title_tag
132 |     description = og_desc
133 |     return {"title": title, "description": description, "image": og_image}
134 | 
135 | 
136 | def _source_label(url: str) -> str:
137 |     if "note.com" in url:
138 |         return "note（ノート）"
139 |     if "prtimes.jp" in url:
140 |         return "PR TIMES"
141 |     return "update"
142 | 
143 | 
144 | def _source_icon(url: str) -> str:
145 |     if "note.com" in url:
146 |         return "https://play-lh.googleusercontent.com/Jcdw4nXOdeg3pMPJldirClrj__oBd-UVZPehnb9Zn5MtWvWCQivgLqJ1mux0JjyxvA"
147 |     if "prtimes.jp" in url:
148 |         return "https://encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com/images?q=tbn:ANd9GcTj6jiPk40UN8Vih6Vtaqv0DiIIF3ZUXWms1g&s"
149 |     return ""
150 | 
151 | 
152 | def send_with_preview(url: str, *, name: str | None = None) -> requests.Response:
153 |     """Send a Slack message with manual preview via blocks."""
154 |     meta = fetch_preview(url)
155 |     title = meta.get("title") or url
156 |     description = meta.get("description") or ""
157 |     image = meta.get("image") or ""
158 | 
159 |     if len(description) > 500:
160 |         description = description[:500] + "…"
161 | 
162 |     label = _source_label(url)
163 |     quoted_lines = []
164 |     label_text = f"{label} - {name}" if name else label
165 |     quoted_lines.append(f"> {label_text}")
166 |     quoted_lines.append(f"> {url}")
167 |     quoted_lines.append(f"> *{title}*")
168 |     if description:
169 |         quoted_lines.append(f"> {description}")
170 | 
171 |     accessory = (
172 |         {"type": "image", "image_url": image, "alt_text": title or "preview"} if image else None
173 |     )
174 | 
175 |     blocks: List[Dict[str, Any]] = [
176 |         {
177 |             "type": "section",
178 |             "text": {"type": "mrkdwn", "text": "\n".join(quoted_lines)},
179 |             **({"accessory": accessory} if accessory else {}),
180 |         }
181 |     ]
182 | 
183 |     fallback_text = f"{name} | {label}: {title}" if name else f"{label}: {title}"
184 |     return send_to_slack(fallback_text, enable_unfurl=False, blocks=blocks)
185 | 
186 | 
187 | def run_notification(window_hours: int = 1, *, persist_cache: bool = True) -> dict:
188 |     """
189 |     Execute release/note checks and post results to Slack.
190 |     Returns a summary dict for logging/HTTP responses.
191 |     """
192 |     sheet_data = load_spreadsheet_data(force_refresh=True, persist=persist_cache)
193 |     name_index = _build_name_index(sheet_data)
194 | 
195 |     pr_data = pr_checker.check_releases(window_hours=window_hours)
196 |     pr_checker.write_output(pr_data)
197 | 
198 |     note_data = note_checker.check_notes(window_hours=window_hours)
199 |     note_checker.write_output(note_data)
200 | 
201 |     pr_entries = [
202 |         (r.get("url", ""), name_index["pr"].get(r.get("company_id", "")))
203 |         for r in pr_data.get("releases", [])
204 |         if r.get("url")
205 |     ]
206 |     note_entries = [
207 |         (a.get("url", ""), name_index["note"].get(a.get("note_id", "")))
208 |         for a in note_data.get("articles", [])
209 |         if a.get("url")
210 |     ]
211 | 
212 |     responses = []
213 |     for url, name in pr_entries:
214 |         responses.append(send_with_preview(url, name=name))
215 |     for url, name in note_entries:
216 |         responses.append(send_with_preview(url, name=name))
217 | 
218 |     # When nothing new, don't post anything.
219 | 
220 |     summary = {
221 |         "pr_count": len(pr_entries),
222 |         "note_count": len(note_entries),
223 |         "messages_sent": len(responses),
224 |         "pr_ids": pr_data.get("target_ids", []),
225 |         "note_ids": note_data.get("target_ids", []),
226 |     }
227 |     return summary
228 | 
229 | 
230 | def main():
231 |     summary = run_notification(window_hours=1)
232 |     print(
233 |         f"Slack posted: pr_count={summary['pr_count']}, "
234 |         f"note_count={summary['note_count']}, "
235 |         f"messages_sent={summary['messages_sent']}"
236 |     )
237 | 
238 | 
239 | def build_response() -> Dict[str, Any]:
240 |     """Shared HTTP response builder for serverless/handler entrypoints."""
241 |     try:
242 |         summary = run_notification(window_hours=1)
243 |         body = {"ok": True, **summary}
244 |         status_code = 200
245 |     except requests.HTTPError as exc:
246 |         body = {"ok": False, "error": f"Slack HTTP error: {exc}"}
247 |         status_code = 502
248 |     except Exception as exc:
249 |         body = {"ok": False, "error": str(exc)}
250 |         status_code = 500
251 | 
252 |     return {
253 |         "statusCode": status_code,
254 |         "headers": {"Content-Type": "application/json"},
255 |         "body": json.dumps(body, ensure_ascii=False),
256 |     }
257 | 
258 | 
259 | class Handler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
260 |     """
261 |     Fallback handler for environments that expect BaseHTTPRequestHandler subclass.
262 |     Delegates to build_response above.
263 |     """
264 | 
265 |     def _dispatch(self):
266 |         result = build_response()
267 |         status = result.get("statusCode", 200)
268 |         headers = result.get("headers", {}) or {}
269 |         body = result.get("body", "")
270 |         if not isinstance(body, (str, bytes)):
271 |             body = json.dumps(body, ensure_ascii=False)
272 | 
273 |         self.send_response(status)
274 |         for k, v in headers.items():
275 |             self.send_header(k, v)
276 |         self.end_headers()
277 |         payload = body if isinstance(body, bytes) else body.encode("utf-8")
278 |         self.wfile.write(payload)
279 | 
280 |     def do_GET(self):
281 |         self._dispatch()
282 | 
283 |     def do_POST(self):
284 |         self._dispatch()
285 | 
286 | 
287 | # Alias expected by older Vercel Python runtimes: treat `handler` as a Handler subclass.
288 | handler = Handler
289 | 
290 | 
291 | if __name__ == "__main__":
292 |     main()
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

 43 |             x_by_id[x_id] = name
 44 | 
 45 |     return {"pr": pr_by_id, "note": note_by_id, "x": x_by_id}
 46 | 
 47 | 
 48 | def _load_env_file():
 49 |     """
 50 |     Minimal .env loader for local runs (KEY="value" lines only).
 51 |     Safe to call in any environment.
 52 |     """
 53 |     env_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / ".env"
 54 |     if not env_path.exists():
 55 |         return
 56 | 
 57 |     with env_path.open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
 58 |         for line in f:
 59 |             line = line.strip()
 60 |             if not line or line.startswith("#") or "=" not in line:
 61 |                 continue
 62 |             key, val = line.split("=", 1)
 63 |             val = val.strip().strip('"').strip("'")
 64 |             os.environ.setdefault(key, val)
 65 | 
 66 | 
 67 | _load_env_file()
 68 | 
 69 | SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL = os.environ.get("SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL")
 70 | HTTP_HEADERS = {
 71 |     "User-Agent": (
 72 |         "Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_15_7) "
 73 |         "AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) "
 74 |         "Chrome/124.0.0.0 Safari/537.36"
 75 |     )
 76 | }
 77 | 
 78 | 
 79 | def send_to_slack(
 80 |     text: str,
 81 |     *,
 82 |     enable_unfurl: bool = True,
 83 |     blocks: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None,
 84 | ) -> requests.Response:
 85 |     if not SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL:
 86 |         raise RuntimeError("SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL is not set")
 87 | 
 88 |     payload = {
 89 |         "text": text,
 90 |         "unfurl_links": enable_unfurl,
 91 |         "unfurl_media": enable_unfurl,
 92 |     }
 93 |     if blocks:
 94 |         payload["blocks"] = blocks
 95 | 
 96 |     resp = requests.post(SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL, json=payload, timeout=10)
 97 |     resp.raise_for_status()
 98 |     return resp
 99 | 
100 | 
101 | def _extract_meta(content: str, pattern: str) -> str:
102 |     """Return first capture from regex pattern (dotall)."""
103 |     m = re.search(pattern, content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
104 |     return (m.group(1).strip() if m else "").replace("\n", " ").strip()
105 | 
106 | 
107 | def fetch_preview(url: str) -> Dict[str, str]:
108 |     """
109 |     Fetch page and extract og:title / og:description / title.
110 |     Best-effort; returns empty strings on failure.
111 |     """
112 |     try:
113 |         resp = requests.get(url, headers=HTTP_HEADERS, timeout=10)
114 |         resp.raise_for_status()
115 |         resp.encoding = resp.apparent_encoding or resp.encoding or "utf-8"
116 |         html = resp.text
117 |     except Exception:
118 |         return {"title": "", "description": ""}
119 | 
120 |     og_title = _extract_meta(
121 |         html, r'<meta[^>]+property=["\']og:title["\'][^>]+content=["\'](.*?)["\']'
122 |     )
123 |     og_desc = _extract_meta(
124 |         html, r'<meta[^>]+property=["\']og:description["\'][^>]+content=["\'](.*?)["\']'
125 |     )
126 |     og_image = _extract_meta(
127 |         html, r'<meta[^>]+property=["\']og:image["\'][^>]+content=["\'](.*?)["\']'
128 |     )
129 |     title_tag = _extract_meta(html, r"<title[^>]*>(.*?)</title>")
130 | 
131 |     title = og_title or title_tag
132 |     description = og_desc
133 |     return {"title": title, "description": description, "image": og_image}
134 | 
135 | 
136 | def _source_label(url: str) -> str:
137 |     if "note.com" in url:
138 |         return "note（ノート）"
139 |     if "prtimes.jp" in url:
140 |         return "PR TIMES"
141 |     return "update"
142 | 
143 | 
144 | def _source_icon(url: str) -> str:
145 |     if "note.com" in url:
146 |         return "https://play-lh.googleusercontent.com/Jcdw4nXOdeg3pMPJldirClrj__oBd-UVZPehnb9Zn5MtWvWCQivgLqJ1mux0JjyxvA"
147 |     if "prtimes.jp" in url:
148 |         return "https://encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com/images?q=tbn:ANd9GcTj6jiPk40UN8Vih6Vtaqv0DiIIF3ZUXWms1g&s"
149 |     return ""
150 | 
151 | 
152 | def send_with_preview(url: str, *, name: str | None = None) -> requests.Response:
153 |     """Send a Slack message with manual preview via blocks."""
154 |     meta = fetch_preview(url)
155 |     title = meta.get("title") or url
156 |     description = meta.get("description") or ""
157 |     image = meta.get("image") or ""
158 | 
159 |     if len(description) > 500:
160 |         description = description[:500] + "…"
161 | 
162 |     label = _source_label(url)
163 |     quoted_lines = []
164 |     label_text = f"{label} - {name}" if name else label
165 |     quoted_lines.append(f"> {label_text}")
166 |     quoted_lines.append(f"> {url}")
167 |     quoted_lines.append(f"> *{title}*")
168 |     if description:
169 |         quoted_lines.append(f"> {description}")
170 | 
171 |     accessory = (
172 |         {"type": "image", "image_url": image, "alt_text": title or "preview"} if image else None
173 |     )
174 | 
175 |     blocks: List[Dict[str, Any]] = [
176 |         {
177 |             "type": "section",
178 |             "text": {"type": "mrkdwn", "text": "\n".join(quoted_lines)},
179 |             **({"accessory": accessory} if accessory else {}),
180 |         }
181 |     ]
182 | 
183 |     fallback_text = f"{name} | {label}: {title}" if name else f"{label}: {title}"
184 |     return send_to_slack(fallback_text, enable_unfurl=False, blocks=blocks)
185 | 
186 | 
187 | def run_notification(window_hours: int = 1, *, persist_cache: bool = True) -> dict:
188 |     """
189 |     Execute release/note checks and post results to Slack.
190 |     Returns a summary dict for logging/HTTP responses.
191 |     """
192 |     sheet_data = load_spreadsheet_data(force_refresh=True, persist=persist_cache)
193 |     name_index = _build_name_index(sheet_data)
194 | 
195 |     pr_data = pr_checker.check_releases(window_hours=window_hours)
196 |     pr_checker.write_output(pr_data)
197 | 
198 |     note_data = note_checker.check_notes(window_hours=window_hours)
199 |     note_checker.write_output(note_data)
200 | 
201 |     pr_entries = [
202 |         (r.get("url", ""), name_index["pr"].get(r.get("company_id", "")))
203 |         for r in pr_data.get("releases", [])
204 |         if r.get("url")
205 |     ]
206 |     note_entries = [
207 |         (a.get("url", ""), name_index["note"].get(a.get("note_id", "")))
208 |         for a in note_data.get("articles", [])
209 |         if a.get("url")
210 |     ]
211 | 
212 |     responses = []
213 |     for url, name in pr_entries:
214 |         responses.append(send_with_preview(url, name=name))
215 |     for url, name in note_entries:
216 |         responses.append(send_with_preview(url, name=name))
217 | 
218 |     # When nothing new, don't post anything.
219 | 
220 |     summary = {
221 |         "pr_count": len(pr_entries),
222 |         "note_count": len(note_entries),
223 |         "messages_sent": len(responses),
224 |         "pr_ids": pr_data.get("target_ids", []),
225 |         "note_ids": note_data.get("target_ids", []),
226 |     }
227 |     return summary
228 | 
229 | 
230 | def main():
231 |     summary = run_notification(window_hours=1)
232 |     print(
233 |         f"Slack posted: pr_count={summary['pr_count']}, "
234 |         f"note_count={summary['note_count']}, "
235 |         f"messages_sent={summary['messages_sent']}"
236 |     )
237 | 
238 | 
239 | def build_response() -> Dict[str, Any]:
240 |     """Shared HTTP response builder for serverless/handler entrypoints."""
241 |     try:
242 |         summary = run_notification(window_hours=1)
243 |         body = {"ok": True, **summary}
244 |         status_code = 200
245 |     except requests.HTTPError as exc:
246 |         body = {"ok": False, "error": f"Slack HTTP error: {exc}"}
247 |         status_code = 502
248 |     except Exception as exc:
249 |         body = {"ok": False, "error": str(exc)}
250 |         status_code = 500
251 | 
252 |     return {
253 |         "statusCode": status_code,
254 |         "headers": {"Content-Type": "application/json"},
255 |         "body": json.dumps(body, ensure_ascii=False),
256 |     }
257 | 
258 | 
259 | class Handler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
260 |     """
261 |     Fallback handler for environments that expect BaseHTTPRequestHandler subclass.
262 |     Delegates to build_response above.
263 |     """
264 | 
265 |     def _dispatch(self):
266 |         result = build_response()
267 |         status = result.get("statusCode", 200)
268 |         headers = result.get("headers", {}) or {}
269 |         body = result.get("body", "")
270 |         if not isinstance(body, (str, bytes)):
271 |             body = json.dumps(body, ensure_ascii=False)
272 | 
273 |         self.send_response(status)
274 |         for k, v in headers.items():
275 |             self.send_header(k, v)
276 |         self.end_headers()
277 |         payload = body if isinstance(body, bytes) else body.encode("utf-8")
278 |         self.wfile.write(payload)
279 | 
280 |     def do_GET(self):
281 |         self._dispatch()
282 | 
283 |     def do_POST(self):
284 |         self._dispatch()
285 | 
286 | 
287 | # Alias expected by older Vercel Python runtimes: treat `handler` as a Handler subclass.
288 | handler = Handler
289 | 
290 | 
291 | if __name__ == "__main__":
292 |     main()
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

 44 |         status = HTTPStatus.BAD_GATEWAY

/.vercel/cache/b_send_slack/dump.py:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1 | import os
  2 | import re
  3 | import fnmatch
  4 | 
  5 | def should_exclude(file_path):
  6 |     """
  7 |     指定されたファイルパスが除外対象かどうかを判定する
  8 |     """
  9 |     # 除外パターンのリスト
 10 |     exclude_patterns = [
 11 |         # Pythonバイトコード
 12 |         "__pycache__/*", "*.py[cod]", "*$py.class",
 13 |         # 環境設定ファイル
 14 |         ".env", "*.json", ".venv/*", "venv/*", "result/*", "ENV/*", "env.bak/*", "env/*",
 15 |         # pip環境設定ファイル
 16 |         "pip-log.txt", "pip-delete-this-directory.txt",
 17 |         # コンパイル成果物
 18 |         "*.egg-info/*", "*.egg", "*.eggs", "*.whl","*.git",".git",
 19 |         # テストカバレッジレポート
 20 |         "htmlcov/*", ".tox/*", ".nox/*", ".coverage", "coverage.*", ".cache",
 21 |         "nosetests.xml", "coverage.xml", "*.cover", "*.py,cover",
 22 |         # Jupyter Notebookのチェックポイント
 23 |         ".ipynb_checkpoints/*",
 24 |         # pylint, mypyなどの設定
 25 |         ".mypy_cache/*", ".pyre/*", ".pytype/*", ".pyright/*",
 26 |         # IDEやエディタの設定ファイル
 27 |         ".vscode/*", ".idea/*", "*.sublime-workspace", "*.sublime-project",
 28 |         # MacやLinuxのシステムファイル
 29 |         ".DS_Store", "*.swp", "*~",
 30 |         # パッケージ管理ツールの成果物
 31 |         "poetry.lock", "Pipfile.lock",
 32 |         # Vercelキャッシュ
 33 |         ".vercel/*", "*/.vercel/*",
 34 |         # Docker関連
 35 |         "docker-compose.override.yml", ".dockerignore",
 36 |         # その他
 37 |         "*.log", "*.pot", "*.mo", "cline_log.txt", "git_tracking_status.txt",
 38 |         # 本番環境用秘密ファイル
 39 |         "*.pem", ".secrets", ".env.act",
 40 |         # 出力ファイル自体を除外
 41 |         "dump_result.txt"
 42 |     ]
 43 | 
 44 |     # ファイル名のみを取得
 45 |     file_name = os.path.basename(file_path)
 46 | 
 47 |     # ディレクトリパスを含む相対パス
 48 |     rel_path = file_path
 49 | 
 50 |     # 除外パターンとマッチするかチェック
 51 |     for pattern in exclude_patterns:
 52 |         # ファイル名だけでマッチングを試みる
 53 |         if fnmatch.fnmatch(file_name, pattern):
 54 |             return True
 55 | 
 56 |         # パスを含めたマッチングも試みる
 57 |         if fnmatch.fnmatch(rel_path, pattern):
 58 |             return True
 59 | 
 60 |         # __pycache__ ディレクトリ内のファイルを除外
 61 |         if "__pycache__" in rel_path:
 62 |             return True
 63 | 
 64 |     # .git 配下は丸ごと除外
 65 |     if rel_path.startswith(".git") or "/.git/" in rel_path:
 66 |         return True
 67 |     # .vecel 配下は丸ごと除外
 68 |     if rel_path.startswith(".vecel") or "/.vecel/" in rel_path:
 69 |         return True
 70 | 
 71 |     return False
 72 | 
 73 | def dump_files_to_txt(target_dir):
 74 |     """
 75 |     指定されたディレクトリ内のファイルを走査し、内容をdump_result.txtに出力する
 76 |     除外リストに含まれるファイルはスキップする
 77 |     フォルダとファイルはアルファベット順にソートされる
 78 |     """
 79 |     # 出力ファイルのパスを指定されたディレクトリ内に設定
 80 |     output_file = os.path.join(target_dir, "dump_result.txt")
 81 | 
 82 |     # 相対パスの基準となるディレクトリ
 83 |     base_dir = target_dir
 84 | 
 85 |     # ファイルパスを収集してソートする
 86 |     all_files = []
 87 |     for root, dirs, files in os.walk(target_dir):
 88 |         # .git など除外ディレクトリを辿らないようフィルタ
 89 |         dirs[:] = [d for d in dirs if not should_exclude(os.path.relpath(os.path.join(root, d), start=base_dir))]
 90 |         # ディレクトリをアルファベット順にソート
 91 |         dirs.sort()
 92 |         # ファイルをアルファベット順にソート
 93 |         for fname in sorted(files):
 94 |             abs_path = os.path.join(root, fname)
 95 | 
 96 |             # 出力ファイル自体はスキップ
 97 |             if abs_path == output_file:
 98 |                 continue
 99 | 
100 |             # 除外対象のファイルはスキップ
101 |             rel_path = os.path.relpath(abs_path, start=base_dir)
102 |             if should_exclude(rel_path):
103 |                 continue
104 | 
105 |             all_files.append((rel_path, abs_path))
106 | 
107 |     # ファイルをアルファベット順にソート
108 |     all_files.sort()
109 | 
110 |     with open(output_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out:
111 |         for rel_path, abs_path in all_files:
112 |             out.write(f"/{rel_path}:\n")
113 |             out.write("-" * 80 + "\n")
114 |             try:
115 |                 with open(abs_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
116 |                     for i, line in enumerate(f, 1):
117 |                         out.write(f"{i:3} | {line.rstrip()}\n")
118 |             except Exception as e:
119 |                 out.write(f"[ERROR READING FILE]: {e}\n")
120 |             out.write("-" * 80 + "\n\n")
121 | 
122 | if __name__ == "__main__":
123 |     # コマンドライン引数を使わず、直接変数に値を設定
124 |     # target_directory = "/Users/neromehiro/hiro folder/my_Works/programing/aimsales-api/module/b_AddCompany"
125 |     target_directory = "/Users/neromehiro/hiro folder/my_Works/programing/makers_release"
126 |     dump_files_to_txt(target_directory)
127 |     print(f"✅ 出力完了: {target_directory}/dump_result.txt")
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

/.vercel/cache/b_send_slack/spreadsheet2json.py:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1 | """
  2 | Fetches public Google Sheet data and writes prtimes_id / note_id lists to JSON.
  3 | 
  4 | The target sheet is shared read-only, so we can download it as CSV via the
  5 | `export?format=csv` endpoint. IDs marked as 'なし' or left blank are skipped.
  6 | 氏名列も含めて取得し、氏名ごとのID情報もJSONに含める。
  7 | """
  8 | 
  9 | from __future__ import annotations
 10 | 
 11 | import csv
 12 | import json
 13 | from pathlib import Path
 14 | from typing import Iterable, List, Mapping
 15 | from urllib.parse import urlparse
 16 | 
 17 | import requests
 18 | 
 19 | 
 20 | SHEET_ID = "16zcRbmvBEdkWDARh-OZ_r8b-ucnKoFKQNLp9nF3EV4k"
 21 | SHEET_GID = "0"
 22 | CSV_URL = (
 23 |     f"https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/{SHEET_ID}/export?format=csv&gid={SHEET_GID}"
 24 | )
 25 | OUTPUT_PATH = Path("spreadsheet2json.json")
 26 | 
 27 | 
 28 | def _dedupe_preserve_order(values: Iterable[str]) -> List[str]:
 29 |     seen = set()
 30 |     unique = []
 31 |     for value in values:
 32 |         if value not in seen:
 33 |             seen.add(value)
 34 |             unique.append(value)
 35 |     return unique
 36 | 
 37 | 
 38 | def _is_missing(value: str) -> bool:
 39 |     normalized = value.replace("　", "").strip()
 40 |     return bool(
 41 |         not normalized
 42 |         or normalized in {"なし", "ナシ", "無し"}
 43 |         or normalized.startswith(("なし", "ナシ", "無し"))
 44 |     )
 45 | 
 46 | 
 47 | def _normalize_note_id(value: str) -> str:
 48 |     """Return just the note username/slug from a URL or raw value."""
 49 |     raw = value.strip()
 50 |     if not raw:
 51 |         return ""
 52 | 
 53 |     # Accept URLs such as https://note.com/username or note.com/@username
 54 |     if "note.com" in raw or "://" in raw:
 55 |         parsed = urlparse(raw if "://" in raw else f"https://{raw}")
 56 |         path = parsed.path.strip("/")
 57 |         if not path:
 58 |             return ""
 59 |         raw = path.split("/")[0]
 60 | 
 61 |     # Strip leading @ if present (note profile pages sometimes include it)
 62 |     return raw.lstrip("@")
 63 | 
 64 | 
 65 | def fetch_sheet_csv() -> str:
 66 |     resp = requests.get(CSV_URL, timeout=30)
 67 |     resp.raise_for_status()
 68 |     resp.encoding = "utf-8"
 69 |     return resp.text
 70 | 
 71 | 
 72 | def parse_ids(csv_text: str) -> dict:
 73 |     prtimes_ids = []
 74 |     note_ids = []
 75 |     x_ids = []
 76 |     people = {}
 77 | 
 78 |     reader = csv.DictReader(csv_text.splitlines())
 79 |     for row in reader:
 80 |         name = (row.get("氏名") or "").strip()
 81 |         prtimes_value = (row.get("prtimes_id") or "").strip()
 82 |         note_value = (row.get("note_id") or "").strip()
 83 |         x_value = (row.get("x_id") or "").strip()
 84 | 
 85 |         person_entry = {}
 86 | 
 87 |         if not _is_missing(prtimes_value):
 88 |             prtimes_ids.append(prtimes_value)
 89 |             person_entry["prtimes_id"] = prtimes_value
 90 |         if not _is_missing(note_value):
 91 |             normalized = _normalize_note_id(note_value)
 92 |             if normalized:
 93 |                 note_ids.append(normalized)
 94 |                 person_entry["note_id"] = normalized
 95 |         if not _is_missing(x_value):
 96 |             x_ids.append(x_value)
 97 |             person_entry["x_id"] = x_value
 98 | 
 99 |         if name:
100 |             people[name] = person_entry
101 | 
102 |     return {
103 |         "prtimes_id": _dedupe_preserve_order(prtimes_ids),
104 |         "note_id": _dedupe_preserve_order(note_ids),
105 |         "x_id": _dedupe_preserve_order(x_ids),
106 |         "by_name": people,
107 |     }
108 | 
109 | 
110 | def write_json(data: dict) -> None:
111 |     text = json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
112 |     try:
113 |         OUTPUT_PATH.write_text(text, encoding="utf-8")
114 |     except OSError:
115 |         # Skip persisting when filesystem is read-only (e.g., serverless)
116 |         pass
117 | 
118 | 
119 | def load_spreadsheet_data(
120 |     force_refresh: bool = False, *, persist: bool = True
121 | ) -> Mapping[str, List[str]]:
122 |     """
123 |     Return parsed sheet data, optionally re-fetching the latest CSV.
124 | 
125 |     Falls back to re-fetching if the on-disk JSON is missing or invalid.
126 |     When persist=False (e.g., API on read-only FS), skip writing the JSON cache.
127 |     """
128 |     if not force_refresh and OUTPUT_PATH.exists():
129 |         try:
130 |             return json.loads(OUTPUT_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
131 |         except Exception:
132 |             pass
133 | 
134 |     csv_text = fetch_sheet_csv()
135 |     data = parse_ids(csv_text)
136 |     if persist:
137 |         write_json(data)
138 |     return data
139 | 
140 | 
141 | def main() -> None:
142 |     csv_text = fetch_sheet_csv()
143 |     data = parse_ids(csv_text)
144 |     write_json(data)
145 | 
146 | 
147 | if __name__ == "__main__":
148 |     main()

 38 |     def fix_tuple_repr(obj):
 39 |         def __repr__(self):
 40 |             cls = self.__class__
 41 |             return "%s(%s)" % (
 42 |                 cls.__name__,
 43 |                 ", ".join(
 44 |                     "%s=%r" % (field, self[index])
 45 |                     for index, field in enumerate(cls._fields)
 46 |                 ),
 47 |             )
 48 | 
 49 |         obj.__repr__ = __repr__
 50 |         return obj

 87 |         rules, upgrades to unicode are implicit.

171 |     def normalize_string_tuple(tup):
172 |         """Ensures that all types in the tuple are either strings
173 |         or bytes.
174 |         """
175 |         tupiter = iter(tup)
176 |         is_text = isinstance(next(tupiter, None), text_type)
177 |         for arg in tupiter:
178 |             if isinstance(arg, text_type) != is_text:
179 |                 raise TypeError(
180 |                     "Cannot mix str and bytes arguments (got %s)" % repr(tup)
181 |                 )

188 |         return s.encode("latin1").decode(charset, errors)
189 |

191 |         if isinstance(s, text_type):
192 |             s = s.encode(charset)
193 |         return s.decode("latin1", errors)

195 |     def to_bytes(x, charset=sys.getdefaultencoding(), errors="strict"):
196 |         if x is None:
197 |             return None
198 |         if isinstance(x, (bytes, bytearray, memoryview)):  # noqa
199 |             return bytes(x)
200 |         if isinstance(x, str):
201 |             return x.encode(charset, errors)
202 |         raise TypeError("Expected bytes")

205 |         if x is None or isinstance(x, str):

210 | def to_unicode(
211 |     x, charset=sys.getdefaultencoding(), errors="strict", allow_none_charset=False
212 | ):
213 |     if x is None:
214 |         return None
215 |     if not isinstance(x, bytes):
216 |         return text_type(x)
217 |     if charset is None and allow_none_charset:
218 |         return x
219 |     return x.decode(charset, errors)

 27 | _logger = None
 28 | _signature_cache = WeakKeyDictionary()
 29 | _epoch_ord = date(1970, 1, 1).toordinal()
 30 | _legal_cookie_chars = (
 31 |     string.ascii_letters + string.digits + u"/=!#$%&'*+-.^_`|~:"
 32 | ).encode("ascii")
 33 | 
 34 | _cookie_quoting_map = {b",": b"\\054", b";": b"\\073", b'"': b'\\"', b"\\": b"\\\\"}
 35 | for _i in chain(range_type(32), range_type(127, 256)):
 36 |     _cookie_quoting_map[int_to_byte(_i)] = ("\\%03o" % _i).encode("latin1")
 37 | 
 38 | _octal_re = re.compile(br"\\[0-3][0-7][0-7]")
 39 | _quote_re = re.compile(br"[\\].")
 40 | _legal_cookie_chars_re = br"[\w\d!#%&\'~_`><@,:/\$\*\+\-\.\^\|\)\(\?\}\{\=]"
 41 | _cookie_re = re.compile(
 42 |     br"""
 43 |     (?P<key>[^=;]+)
 44 |     (?:\s*=\s*
 45 |         (?P<val>
 46 |             "(?:[^\\"]|\\.)*" |
 47 |              (?:.*?)
 48 |         )
 49 |     )?
 50 |     \s*;
 51 | """,
 52 |     flags=re.VERBOSE,

 56 | class _Missing(object):
 57 |     def __repr__(self):
 58 |         return "no value"
 59 | 
 60 |     def __reduce__(self):
 61 |         return "_missing"

 67 | def _get_environ(obj):
 68 |     env = getattr(obj, "environ", obj)
 69 |     assert isinstance(env, dict), (
 70 |         "%r is not a WSGI environment (has to be a dict)" % type(obj).__name__
 71 |     )
 72 |     return env

 74 | 
 75 | def _has_level_handler(logger):
 76 |     """Check if there is a handler in the logging chain that will handle
 77 |     the given logger's effective level.
 78 |     """
 79 |     level = logger.getEffectiveLevel()
 80 |     current = logger
 81 | 
 82 |     while current:
 83 |         if any(handler.level <= level for handler in current.handlers):
 84 |             return True
 85 | 
 86 |         if not current.propagate:
 87 |             break
 88 | 
 89 |         current = current.parent
 90 | 
 91 |     return False
 92 | 
 93 | 
 94 | def _log(type, message, *args, **kwargs):
 95 |     """Log a message to the 'werkzeug' logger.
 96 | 
 97 |     The logger is created the first time it is needed. If there is no
 98 |     level set, it is set to :data:`logging.INFO`. If there is no handler
 99 |     for the logger's effective level, a :class:`logging.StreamHandler`
100 |     is added.
101 |     """
102 |     global _logger
103 | 
104 |     if _logger is None:
105 |         _logger = logging.getLogger("werkzeug")
106 | 
107 |         if _logger.level == logging.NOTSET:
108 |             _logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)
109 | 
110 |         if not _has_level_handler(_logger):
111 |             _logger.addHandler(logging.StreamHandler())
112 | 
113 |     getattr(_logger, type)(message.rstrip(), *args, **kwargs)
114 | 
115 | 
116 | def _parse_signature(func):
117 |     """Return a signature object for the function."""
118 |     if hasattr(func, "im_func"):
119 |         func = func.im_func
120 | 
121 |     # if we have a cached validator for this function, return it
122 |     parse = _signature_cache.get(func)
123 |     if parse is not None:
124 |         return parse
125 | 
126 |     # inspect the function signature and collect all the information
127 |     if hasattr(inspect, "getfullargspec"):
128 |         tup = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
129 |     else:
130 |         tup = inspect.getargspec(func)
131 |     positional, vararg_var, kwarg_var, defaults = tup[:4]
132 |     defaults = defaults or ()
133 |     arg_count = len(positional)
134 |     arguments = []
135 |     for idx, name in enumerate(positional):
136 |         if isinstance(name, list):
137 |             raise TypeError(
138 |                 "cannot parse functions that unpack tuples in the function signature"
139 |             )
140 |         try:
141 |             default = defaults[idx - arg_count]
142 |         except IndexError:
143 |             param = (name, False, None)
144 |         else:
145 |             param = (name, True, default)
146 |         arguments.append(param)
147 |     arguments = tuple(arguments)
148 | 
149 |     def parse(args, kwargs):
150 |         new_args = []
151 |         missing = []
152 |         extra = {}
153 | 
154 |         # consume as many arguments as positional as possible
155 |         for idx, (name, has_default, default) in enumerate(arguments):
156 |             try:
157 |                 new_args.append(args[idx])
158 |             except IndexError:
159 |                 try:
160 |                     new_args.append(kwargs.pop(name))
161 |                 except KeyError:
162 |                     if has_default:
163 |                         new_args.append(default)
164 |                     else:
165 |                         missing.append(name)
166 |             else:
167 |                 if name in kwargs:
168 |                     extra[name] = kwargs.pop(name)
169 | 
170 |         # handle extra arguments
171 |         extra_positional = args[arg_count:]
172 |         if vararg_var is not None:
173 |             new_args.extend(extra_positional)
174 |             extra_positional = ()
175 |         if kwargs and kwarg_var is None:
176 |             extra.update(kwargs)
177 |             kwargs = {}
178 | 
179 |         return (
180 |             new_args,
181 |             kwargs,
182 |             missing,
183 |             extra,
184 |             extra_positional,
185 |             arguments,
186 |             vararg_var,
187 |             kwarg_var,
188 |         )
189 | 
190 |     _signature_cache[func] = parse
191 |     return parse
192 | 
193 | 
194 | def _date_to_unix(arg):
195 |     """Converts a timetuple, integer or datetime object into the seconds from
196 |     epoch in utc.
197 |     """
198 |     if isinstance(arg, datetime):
199 |         arg = arg.utctimetuple()
200 |     elif isinstance(arg, integer_types + (float,)):
201 |         return int(arg)
202 |     year, month, day, hour, minute, second = arg[:6]
203 |     days = date(year, month, 1).toordinal() - _epoch_ord + day - 1
204 |     hours = days * 24 + hour
205 |     minutes = hours * 60 + minute
206 |     seconds = minutes * 60 + second
207 |     return seconds
208 | 
209 | 
210 | class _DictAccessorProperty(object):
211 |     """Baseclass for `environ_property` and `header_property`."""
212 | 
213 |     read_only = False
214 | 
215 |     def __init__(
216 |         self,
217 |         name,
218 |         default=None,
219 |         load_func=None,
220 |         dump_func=None,
221 |         read_only=None,
222 |         doc=None,
223 |     ):
224 |         self.name = name
225 |         self.default = default
226 |         self.load_func = load_func
227 |         self.dump_func = dump_func
228 |         if read_only is not None:
229 |             self.read_only = read_only
230 |         self.__doc__ = doc
231 | 
232 |     def __get__(self, obj, type=None):
233 |         if obj is None:
234 |             return self
235 |         storage = self.lookup(obj)
236 |         if self.name not in storage:
237 |             return self.default
238 |         rv = storage[self.name]
239 |         if self.load_func is not None:
240 |             try:
241 |                 rv = self.load_func(rv)
242 |             except (ValueError, TypeError):
243 |                 rv = self.default
244 |         return rv
245 | 
246 |     def __set__(self, obj, value):
247 |         if self.read_only:
248 |             raise AttributeError("read only property")
249 |         if self.dump_func is not None:
250 |             value = self.dump_func(value)
251 |         self.lookup(obj)[self.name] = value
252 | 
253 |     def __delete__(self, obj):
254 |         if self.read_only:
255 |             raise AttributeError("read only property")
256 |         self.lookup(obj).pop(self.name, None)
257 | 
258 |     def __repr__(self):
259 |         return "<%s %s>" % (self.__class__.__name__, self.name)
260 | 
261 | 
262 | def _cookie_quote(b):
263 |     buf = bytearray()
264 |     all_legal = True
265 |     _lookup = _cookie_quoting_map.get
266 |     _push = buf.extend
267 | 
268 |     for char in iter_bytes(b):
269 |         if char not in _legal_cookie_chars:
270 |             all_legal = False
271 |             char = _lookup(char, char)
272 |         _push(char)
273 | 
274 |     if all_legal:
275 |         return bytes(buf)
276 |     return bytes(b'"' + buf + b'"')
277 | 
278 | 
279 | def _cookie_unquote(b):
280 |     if len(b) < 2:
281 |         return b
282 |     if b[:1] != b'"' or b[-1:] != b'"':
283 |         return b
284 | 
285 |     b = b[1:-1]
286 | 
287 |     i = 0
288 |     n = len(b)
289 |     rv = bytearray()
290 |     _push = rv.extend
291 | 
292 |     while 0 <= i < n:
293 |         o_match = _octal_re.search(b, i)
294 |         q_match = _quote_re.search(b, i)
295 |         if not o_match and not q_match:
296 |             rv.extend(b[i:])
297 |             break
298 |         j = k = -1
299 |         if o_match:
300 |             j = o_match.start(0)
301 |         if q_match:
302 |             k = q_match.start(0)
303 |         if q_match and (not o_match or k < j):
304 |             _push(b[i:k])
305 |             _push(b[k + 1 : k + 2])
306 |             i = k + 2
307 |         else:
308 |             _push(b[i:j])
309 |             rv.append(int(b[j + 1 : j + 4], 8))
310 |             i = j + 4
311 | 
312 |     return bytes(rv)
313 | 
314 | 
315 | def _cookie_parse_impl(b):
316 |     """Lowlevel cookie parsing facility that operates on bytes."""
317 |     i = 0
318 |     n = len(b)
319 | 
320 |     while i < n:
321 |         match = _cookie_re.search(b + b";", i)
322 |         if not match:
323 |             break
324 | 
325 |         key = match.group("key").strip()
326 |         value = match.group("val") or b""
327 |         i = match.end(0)
328 | 
329 |         yield _cookie_unquote(key), _cookie_unquote(value)
330 | 
331 | 
332 | def _encode_idna(domain):
333 |     # If we're given bytes, make sure they fit into ASCII
334 |     if not isinstance(domain, text_type):
335 |         domain.decode("ascii")
336 |         return domain
337 | 
338 |     # Otherwise check if it's already ascii, then return
339 |     try:
340 |         return domain.encode("ascii")
341 |     except UnicodeError:
342 |         pass
343 | 
344 |     # Otherwise encode each part separately
345 |     parts = domain.split(".")
346 |     for idx, part in enumerate(parts):
347 |         parts[idx] = part.encode("idna")
348 |     return b".".join(parts)
349 | 
350 | 
351 | def _decode_idna(domain):
352 |     # If the input is a string try to encode it to ascii to
353 |     # do the idna decoding.  if that fails because of an
354 |     # unicode error, then we already have a decoded idna domain
355 |     if isinstance(domain, text_type):
356 |         try:
357 |             domain = domain.encode("ascii")
358 |         except UnicodeError:
359 |             return domain
360 | 
361 |     # Decode each part separately.  If a part fails, try to
362 |     # decode it with ascii and silently ignore errors.  This makes
363 |     # most sense because the idna codec does not have error handling
364 |     parts = domain.split(b".")
365 |     for idx, part in enumerate(parts):
366 |         try:
367 |             parts[idx] = part.decode("idna")
368 |         except UnicodeError:
369 |             parts[idx] = part.decode("ascii", "ignore")
370 | 
371 |     return ".".join(parts)
372 | 
373 | 
374 | def _make_cookie_domain(domain):
375 |     if domain is None:
376 |         return None
377 |     domain = _encode_idna(domain)
378 |     if b":" in domain:
379 |         domain = domain.split(b":", 1)[0]
380 |     if b"." in domain:
381 |         return domain
382 |     raise ValueError(
383 |         "Setting 'domain' for a cookie on a server running locally (ex: "
384 |         "localhost) is not supported by complying browsers. You should "
385 |         "have something like: '127.0.0.1 localhost dev.localhost' on "
386 |         "your hosts file and then point your server to run on "
387 |         "'dev.localhost' and also set 'domain' for 'dev.localhost'"
388 |     )

440 |     def easteregged(environ, start_response):

443 |             return start_response(status, headers, exc_info)

448 |         return [
449 |             (
450 |                 u"""
451 | <!DOCTYPE html>
452 | <html>
453 | <head>
454 | <title>About Werkzeug</title>
455 | <style type="text/css">

  1 | import os
  2 | import subprocess
  3 | import sys
  4 | import threading
  5 | import time
  6 | from itertools import chain
  7 | 
  8 | from ._compat import iteritems
  9 | from ._compat import PY2
 10 | from ._compat import text_type
 11 | from ._internal import _log
