 21 | def _build_name_index(sheet: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, str]]:
 22 |     """Create lookup tables to resolve names by prtimes_id / note_id / x_id."""
 23 |     by_name = sheet.get("by_name") if isinstance(sheet, dict) else {}
 24 |     pr_by_id: Dict[str, str] = {}
 25 |     note_by_id: Dict[str, str] = {}
 26 |     x_by_id: Dict[str, str] = {}
 27 |     if not isinstance(by_name, dict):
 28 |         return {"pr": pr_by_id, "note": note_by_id, "x": x_by_id}
 29 | 
 30 |     for name, ids in by_name.items():
 31 |         if not isinstance(ids, dict):
 32 |             continue
 33 |         pr_id = str(ids.get("prtimes_id", "")).strip()
 34 |         if pr_id:
 35 |             pr_by_id[pr_id.lstrip("0") or "0"] = name
 36 | 
 37 |         note_id = str(ids.get("note_id", "")).strip()
 38 |         if note_id:
 39 |             note_by_id[note_id] = name
 40 | 
 41 |         x_id = str(ids.get("x_id", "")).strip()
 42 |         if x_id:
 43 |             x_by_id[x_id] = name
 44 | 
 45 |     return {"pr": pr_by_id, "note": note_by_id, "x": x_by_id}
 46 | 
 47 | 
 48 | def _load_env_file():
 49 |     """
 50 |     Minimal .env loader for local runs (KEY="value" lines only).
 51 |     Safe to call in any environment.
 52 |     """
 53 |     env_path = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / ".env"
 54 |     if not env_path.exists():
 55 |         return
 56 | 
 57 |     with env_path.open(encoding="utf-8") as f:
 58 |         for line in f:
 59 |             line = line.strip()
 60 |             if not line or line.startswith("#") or "=" not in line:
 61 |                 continue
 62 |             key, val = line.split("=", 1)
 63 |             val = val.strip().strip('"').strip("'")
 64 |             os.environ.setdefault(key, val)
 65 |

 69 | SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL = os.environ.get("SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL")
 70 | HTTP_HEADERS = {
 71 |     "User-Agent": (
 72 |         "Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_15_7) "
 73 |         "AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) "
 74 |         "Chrome/124.0.0.0 Safari/537.36"

 79 | def send_to_slack(
 80 |     text: str,
 81 |     *,
 82 |     enable_unfurl: bool = True,
 83 |     blocks: Optional[List[Dict[str, Any]]] = None,
 84 | ) -> requests.Response:
 85 |     if not SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL:
 86 |         raise RuntimeError("SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL is not set")
 87 | 
 88 |     payload = {
 89 |         "text": text,
 90 |         "unfurl_links": enable_unfurl,
 91 |         "unfurl_media": enable_unfurl,
 92 |     }
 93 |     if blocks:
 94 |         payload["blocks"] = blocks
 95 | 
 96 |     resp = requests.post(SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL, json=payload, timeout=10)
 97 |     resp.raise_for_status()
 98 |     return resp
 99 | 
100 | 
101 | def _extract_meta(content: str, pattern: str) -> str:
102 |     """Return first capture from regex pattern (dotall)."""
103 |     m = re.search(pattern, content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
104 |     return (m.group(1).strip() if m else "").replace("\n", " ").strip()
105 | 
106 | 
107 | def fetch_preview(url: str) -> Dict[str, str]:
108 |     """
109 |     Fetch page and extract og:title / og:description / title.
110 |     Best-effort; returns empty strings on failure.
111 |     """
112 |     try:
113 |         resp = requests.get(url, headers=HTTP_HEADERS, timeout=10)
114 |         resp.raise_for_status()
115 |         resp.encoding = resp.apparent_encoding or resp.encoding or "utf-8"
116 |         html = resp.text
117 |     except Exception:
118 |         return {"title": "", "description": ""}
119 | 
120 |     og_title = _extract_meta(
121 |         html, r'<meta[^>]+property=["\']og:title["\'][^>]+content=["\'](.*?)["\']'
122 |     )
123 |     og_desc = _extract_meta(
124 |         html, r'<meta[^>]+property=["\']og:description["\'][^>]+content=["\'](.*?)["\']'
125 |     )
126 |     og_image = _extract_meta(
127 |         html, r'<meta[^>]+property=["\']og:image["\'][^>]+content=["\'](.*?)["\']'
128 |     )
129 |     title_tag = _extract_meta(html, r"<title[^>]*>(.*?)</title>")
130 | 
131 |     title = og_title or title_tag
132 |     description = og_desc
133 |     return {"title": title, "description": description, "image": og_image}
134 | 
135 | 
136 | def _source_label(url: str) -> str:
137 |     if "note.com" in url:
138 |         return "note（ノート）"
139 |     if "prtimes.jp" in url:
140 |         return "PR TIMES"
141 |     return "update"
142 | 
143 | 
144 | def _source_icon(url: str) -> str:
145 |     if "note.com" in url:
146 |         return "https://play-lh.googleusercontent.com/Jcdw4nXOdeg3pMPJldirClrj__oBd-UVZPehnb9Zn5MtWvWCQivgLqJ1mux0JjyxvA"
147 |     if "prtimes.jp" in url:
148 |         return "https://encrypted-tbn0.gstatic.com/images?q=tbn:ANd9GcTj6jiPk40UN8Vih6Vtaqv0DiIIF3ZUXWms1g&s"
149 |     return ""
150 | 
151 | 
152 | def send_with_preview(url: str, *, name: str | None = None) -> requests.Response:
153 |     """Send a Slack message with manual preview via blocks."""
154 |     meta = fetch_preview(url)
155 |     title = meta.get("title") or url
156 |     description = meta.get("description") or ""
157 |     image = meta.get("image") or ""
158 | 
159 |     if len(description) > 500:
160 |         description = description[:500] + "…"
161 | 
162 |     label = _source_label(url)
163 |     quoted_lines = []
164 |     label_text = f"{label} - {name}" if name else label
165 |     quoted_lines.append(f"> {label_text}")
166 |     quoted_lines.append(f"> {url}")
167 |     quoted_lines.append(f"> *{title}*")
168 |     if description:
169 |         quoted_lines.append(f"> {description}")
170 | 
171 |     accessory = (
172 |         {"type": "image", "image_url": image, "alt_text": title or "preview"} if image else None
173 |     )
174 | 
175 |     blocks: List[Dict[str, Any]] = [
176 |         {
177 |             "type": "section",
178 |             "text": {"type": "mrkdwn", "text": "\n".join(quoted_lines)},
179 |             **({"accessory": accessory} if accessory else {}),
180 |         }
181 |     ]
182 | 
183 |     fallback_text = f"{name} | {label}: {title}" if name else f"{label}: {title}"
184 |     return send_to_slack(fallback_text, enable_unfurl=False, blocks=blocks)
185 | 
186 | 
187 | def run_notification(window_hours: int = 1, *, persist_cache: bool = True) -> dict:
188 |     """
189 |     Execute release/note checks and post results to Slack.
190 |     Returns a summary dict for logging/HTTP responses.
191 |     """
192 |     sheet_data = load_spreadsheet_data(force_refresh=True, persist=persist_cache)
193 |     name_index = _build_name_index(sheet_data)
194 | 
195 |     pr_data = pr_checker.check_releases(window_hours=window_hours)
196 |     pr_checker.write_output(pr_data)
197 | 
198 |     note_data = note_checker.check_notes(window_hours=window_hours)
199 |     note_checker.write_output(note_data)
200 | 
201 |     pr_entries = [
202 |         (r.get("url", ""), name_index["pr"].get(r.get("company_id", "")))
203 |         for r in pr_data.get("releases", [])
204 |         if r.get("url")
205 |     ]
206 |     note_entries = [
207 |         (a.get("url", ""), name_index["note"].get(a.get("note_id", "")))
208 |         for a in note_data.get("articles", [])
209 |         if a.get("url")
210 |     ]
211 | 
212 |     responses = []
213 |     for url, name in pr_entries:
214 |         responses.append(send_with_preview(url, name=name))
215 |     for url, name in note_entries:
216 |         responses.append(send_with_preview(url, name=name))
217 | 
218 |     # When nothing new, don't post anything.
219 | 
220 |     summary = {
221 |         "pr_count": len(pr_entries),
222 |         "note_count": len(note_entries),
223 |         "messages_sent": len(responses),
224 |         "pr_ids": pr_data.get("target_ids", []),
225 |         "note_ids": note_data.get("target_ids", []),
226 |     }
227 |     return summary
228 | 
229 | 
230 | def main():
231 |     summary = run_notification(window_hours=1)
232 |     print(
233 |         f"Slack posted: pr_count={summary['pr_count']}, "
234 |         f"note_count={summary['note_count']}, "
235 |         f"messages_sent={summary['messages_sent']}"
236 |     )
237 | 
238 | 
239 | def build_response() -> Dict[str, Any]:
240 |     """Shared HTTP response builder for serverless/handler entrypoints."""
241 |     try:
242 |         summary = run_notification(window_hours=1)
243 |         body = {"ok": True, **summary}
244 |         status_code = 200
245 |     except requests.HTTPError as exc:
246 |         body = {"ok": False, "error": f"Slack HTTP error: {exc}"}
247 |         status_code = 502
248 |     except Exception as exc:
249 |         body = {"ok": False, "error": str(exc)}
250 |         status_code = 500
251 | 
252 |     return {
253 |         "statusCode": status_code,
254 |         "headers": {"Content-Type": "application/json"},
255 |         "body": json.dumps(body, ensure_ascii=False),
256 |     }
257 | 
258 | 
259 | class Handler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
260 |     """
261 |     Fallback handler for environments that expect BaseHTTPRequestHandler subclass.
262 |     Delegates to build_response above.
263 |     """
264 | 
265 |     def _dispatch(self):
266 |         result = build_response()
267 |         status = result.get("statusCode", 200)
268 |         headers = result.get("headers", {}) or {}
269 |         body = result.get("body", "")
270 |         if not isinstance(body, (str, bytes)):
271 |             body = json.dumps(body, ensure_ascii=False)
272 | 
273 |         self.send_response(status)
274 |         for k, v in headers.items():
275 |             self.send_header(k, v)
276 |         self.end_headers()
277 |         payload = body if isinstance(body, bytes) else body.encode("utf-8")
278 |         self.wfile.write(payload)
279 | 
280 |     def do_GET(self):
281 |         self._dispatch()
282 | 
283 |     def do_POST(self):
284 |         self._dispatch()
285 | 
286 | 
287 | # Alias expected by older Vercel Python runtimes: treat `handler` as a Handler subclass.
288 | handler = Handler
289 | 
290 | 
291 | if __name__ == "__main__":
292 |     main()

 35 |     Vercel Python entrypoint.
 36 |     Triggers PR TIMES + note checks and posts results to Slack.
 37 |     """

/dump.py:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1 | import os
  2 | import re
  3 | import fnmatch
  4 | 
  5 | def should_exclude(file_path):
  6 |     """
  7 |     指定されたファイルパスが除外対象かどうかを判定する
  8 |     """
  9 |     # 除外パターンのリスト
 10 |     exclude_patterns = [
 11 |         # Pythonバイトコード
 12 |         "__pycache__/*", "*.py[cod]", "*$py.class",
 13 |         # 環境設定ファイル
 14 |         ".env", "*.json", ".venv/*", "venv/*", "result/*", "ENV/*", "env.bak/*", "env/*",
 15 |         # pip環境設定ファイル
 16 |         "pip-log.txt", "pip-delete-this-directory.txt",
 17 |         # コンパイル成果物
 18 |         "*.egg-info/*", "*.egg", "*.eggs", "*.whl","*.git",".git",
 19 |         # テストカバレッジレポート
 20 |         "htmlcov/*", ".tox/*", ".nox/*", ".coverage", "coverage.*", ".cache",
 21 |         "nosetests.xml", "coverage.xml", "*.cover", "*.py,cover",
 22 |         # Jupyter Notebookのチェックポイント
 23 |         ".ipynb_checkpoints/*",
 24 |         # pylint, mypyなどの設定
 25 |         ".mypy_cache/*", ".pyre/*", ".pytype/*", ".pyright/*",
 26 |         # IDEやエディタの設定ファイル
 27 |         ".vscode/*", ".idea/*", "*.sublime-workspace", "*.sublime-project",
 28 |         # MacやLinuxのシステムファイル
 29 |         ".DS_Store", "*.swp", "*~",
 30 |         # パッケージ管理ツールの成果物
 31 |         "poetry.lock", "Pipfile.lock",
 32 |         # Vercelキャッシュ
 33 |         ".vercel/*", "*/.vercel/*",
 34 |         # Docker関連
 35 |         "docker-compose.override.yml", ".dockerignore",
 36 |         # その他
 37 |         "*.log", "*.pot", "*.mo", "cline_log.txt", "git_tracking_status.txt",
 38 |         # 本番環境用秘密ファイル
 39 |         "*.pem", ".secrets", ".env.act",
 40 |         # 出力ファイル自体を除外
 41 |         "dump_result.txt"
 42 |     ]
 43 | 
 44 |     # ファイル名のみを取得
 45 |     file_name = os.path.basename(file_path)
 46 | 
 47 |     # ディレクトリパスを含む相対パス
 48 |     rel_path = file_path
 49 | 
 50 |     # 除外パターンとマッチするかチェック
 51 |     for pattern in exclude_patterns:
 52 |         # ファイル名だけでマッチングを試みる
 53 |         if fnmatch.fnmatch(file_name, pattern):
 54 |             return True
 55 | 
 56 |         # パスを含めたマッチングも試みる
 57 |         if fnmatch.fnmatch(rel_path, pattern):
 58 |             return True
 59 | 
 60 |         # __pycache__ ディレクトリ内のファイルを除外
 61 |         if "__pycache__" in rel_path:
 62 |             return True
 63 | 
 64 |     # .git 配下は丸ごと除外
 65 |     if rel_path.startswith(".git") or "/.git/" in rel_path:
 66 |         return True
 67 |     # .vecel 配下は丸ごと除外
 68 |     if rel_path.startswith(".vecel") or "/.vecel/" in rel_path:
 69 |         return True
 70 | 
 71 |     return False
 72 | 
 73 | def dump_files_to_txt(target_dir):
 74 |     """
 75 |     指定されたディレクトリ内のファイルを走査し、内容をdump_result.txtに出力する
 76 |     除外リストに含まれるファイルはスキップする
 77 |     フォルダとファイルはアルファベット順にソートされる
 78 |     """
 79 |     # 出力ファイルのパスを指定されたディレクトリ内に設定
 80 |     output_file = os.path.join(target_dir, "dump_result.txt")
 81 | 
 82 |     # 相対パスの基準となるディレクトリ
 83 |     base_dir = target_dir
 84 | 
 85 |     # ファイルパスを収集してソートする
 86 |     all_files = []
 87 |     for root, dirs, files in os.walk(target_dir):
 88 |         # .git など除外ディレクトリを辿らないようフィルタ
 89 |         dirs[:] = [d for d in dirs if not should_exclude(os.path.relpath(os.path.join(root, d), start=base_dir))]
 90 |         # ディレクトリをアルファベット順にソート
 91 |         dirs.sort()
 92 |         # ファイルをアルファベット順にソート
 93 |         for fname in sorted(files):
 94 |             abs_path = os.path.join(root, fname)
 95 | 
 96 |             # 出力ファイル自体はスキップ
 97 |             if abs_path == output_file:
 98 |                 continue
 99 | 
100 |             # 除外対象のファイルはスキップ
101 |             rel_path = os.path.relpath(abs_path, start=base_dir)
102 |             if should_exclude(rel_path):
103 |                 continue
104 | 
105 |             all_files.append((rel_path, abs_path))
106 | 
107 |     # ファイルをアルファベット順にソート
108 |     all_files.sort()
109 | 
110 |     with open(output_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as out:
111 |         for rel_path, abs_path in all_files:
112 |             out.write(f"/{rel_path}:\n")
113 |             out.write("-" * 80 + "\n")
114 |             try:
115 |                 with open(abs_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
116 |                     for i, line in enumerate(f, 1):
117 |                         out.write(f"{i:3} | {line.rstrip()}\n")
118 |             except Exception as e:
119 |                 out.write(f"[ERROR READING FILE]: {e}\n")
120 |             out.write("-" * 80 + "\n\n")
121 | 
122 | if __name__ == "__main__":
123 |     # コマンドライン引数を使わず、直接変数に値を設定
124 |     # target_directory = "/Users/neromehiro/hiro folder/my_Works/programing/aimsales-api/module/b_AddCompany"
125 |     target_directory = "/Users/neromehiro/hiro folder/my_Works/programing/makers_release"
126 |     dump_files_to_txt(target_directory)
127 |     print(f"✅ 出力完了: {target_directory}/dump_result.txt")
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

/spreadsheet2json.py:
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  1 | """
  2 | Fetches public Google Sheet data and writes prtimes_id / note_id lists to JSON.
  3 | 
  4 | The target sheet is shared read-only, so we can download it as CSV via the
  5 | `export?format=csv` endpoint. IDs marked as 'なし' or left blank are skipped.
  6 | 氏名列も含めて取得し、氏名ごとのID情報もJSONに含める。
  7 | """
  8 | 
  9 | from __future__ import annotations
 10 | 
 11 | import csv
 12 | import json
 13 | from pathlib import Path
 14 | from typing import Iterable, List, Mapping
 15 | from urllib.parse import urlparse
 16 | 
 17 | import requests
 18 | 
 19 | 
 20 | SHEET_ID = "16zcRbmvBEdkWDARh-OZ_r8b-ucnKoFKQNLp9nF3EV4k"
 21 | SHEET_GID = "0"
 22 | CSV_URL = (
 23 |     f"https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/{SHEET_ID}/export?format=csv&gid={SHEET_GID}"
 24 | )
 25 | OUTPUT_PATH = Path("spreadsheet2json.json")
 26 | 
 27 | 
 28 | def _dedupe_preserve_order(values: Iterable[str]) -> List[str]:
 29 |     seen = set()
 30 |     unique = []
 31 |     for value in values:
 32 |         if value not in seen:
 33 |             seen.add(value)
 34 |             unique.append(value)
 35 |     return unique
 36 | 
 37 | 
 38 | def _is_missing(value: str) -> bool:
 39 |     normalized = value.replace("　", "").strip()
 40 |     return bool(
 41 |         not normalized
 42 |         or normalized in {"なし", "ナシ", "無し"}
 43 |         or normalized.startswith(("なし", "ナシ", "無し"))
 44 |     )
 45 | 
 46 | 
 47 | def _normalize_note_id(value: str) -> str:
 48 |     """Return just the note username/slug from a URL or raw value."""
 49 |     raw = value.strip()
 50 |     if not raw:
 51 |         return ""
 52 | 
 53 |     # Accept URLs such as https://note.com/username or note.com/@username
 54 |     if "note.com" in raw or "://" in raw:
 55 |         parsed = urlparse(raw if "://" in raw else f"https://{raw}")
 56 |         path = parsed.path.strip("/")
 57 |         if not path:
 58 |             return ""
 59 |         raw = path.split("/")[0]
 60 | 
 61 |     # Strip leading @ if present (note profile pages sometimes include it)
 62 |     return raw.lstrip("@")
 63 | 
 64 | 
 65 | def fetch_sheet_csv() -> str:
 66 |     resp = requests.get(CSV_URL, timeout=30)
 67 |     resp.raise_for_status()
 68 |     resp.encoding = "utf-8"
 69 |     return resp.text
 70 | 
 71 | 
 72 | def parse_ids(csv_text: str) -> dict:
 73 |     prtimes_ids = []
 74 |     note_ids = []
 75 |     x_ids = []
 76 |     people = {}
 77 | 
 78 |     reader = csv.DictReader(csv_text.splitlines())
 79 |     for row in reader:
 80 |         name = (row.get("氏名") or "").strip()
 81 |         prtimes_value = (row.get("prtimes_id") or "").strip()
 82 |         note_value = (row.get("note_id") or "").strip()
 83 |         x_value = (row.get("x_id") or "").strip()
 84 | 
 85 |         person_entry = {}
 86 | 
 87 |         if not _is_missing(prtimes_value):
 88 |             prtimes_ids.append(prtimes_value)
 89 |             person_entry["prtimes_id"] = prtimes_value
 90 |         if not _is_missing(note_value):
 91 |             normalized = _normalize_note_id(note_value)
 92 |             if normalized:
 93 |                 note_ids.append(normalized)
 94 |                 person_entry["note_id"] = normalized
 95 |         if not _is_missing(x_value):
 96 |             x_ids.append(x_value)
 97 |             person_entry["x_id"] = x_value
 98 | 
 99 |         if name:
100 |             people[name] = person_entry
101 | 
102 |     return {
103 |         "prtimes_id": _dedupe_preserve_order(prtimes_ids),
104 |         "note_id": _dedupe_preserve_order(note_ids),
105 |         "x_id": _dedupe_preserve_order(x_ids),
106 |         "by_name": people,
107 |     }
108 | 
109 | 
110 | def write_json(data: dict) -> None:
111 |     text = json.dumps(data, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
112 |     try:
113 |         OUTPUT_PATH.write_text(text, encoding="utf-8")
114 |     except OSError:
115 |         # Skip persisting when filesystem is read-only (e.g., serverless)
116 |         pass
117 | 
118 | 
119 | def load_spreadsheet_data(
120 |     force_refresh: bool = False, *, persist: bool = True
121 | ) -> Mapping[str, List[str]]:
122 |     """
123 |     Return parsed sheet data, optionally re-fetching the latest CSV.
124 | 
125 |     Falls back to re-fetching if the on-disk JSON is missing or invalid.
126 |     When persist=False (e.g., API on read-only FS), skip writing the JSON cache.
127 |     """
128 |     if not force_refresh and OUTPUT_PATH.exists():
129 |         try:
130 |             return json.loads(OUTPUT_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
131 |         except Exception:
132 |             pass
133 | 
134 |     csv_text = fetch_sheet_csv()
135 |     data = parse_ids(csv_text)
136 |     if persist:
137 |         write_json(data)
138 |     return data
139 | 
140 | 
141 | def main() -> None:
142 |     csv_text = fetch_sheet_csv()
143 |     data = parse_ids(csv_text)
144 |     write_json(data)
145 | 
146 | 
147 | if __name__ == "__main__":
148 |     main()
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
